verdict: High risk of AI displacement due to increasing automation in flight operations and instrumentation.

reasoning_steps.title: Increasing automation in flight operations
reasoning_steps.body: The use of autopilot and flight management computers is becoming more prevalent, reducing the need for human pilots to manually control the aircraft.

reasoning_steps.title: Advances in instrumentation
reasoning_steps.body: Improved instrumentation such as GPS and radar are allowing for more accurate navigation and reduced reliance on human pilots to guide flights.

adjacent_careers.name: Air Traffic Controller
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Both pilots and air traffic controllers require strong communication skills and the ability to work well under pressure.

adjacent_careers.name: Flight Dispatcher
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Flight dispatchers and pilots must work together to ensure safe and efficient flight operations.

recent_developments.headline: Boeing to automate pilot tasks with AI
recent_developments.impact: This development highlights the increasing use of automation in flight operations and may reduce the need for human pilots in certain roles.

recent_developments.headline: Anthropic Economic Index maps real AI usage across the workforce
recent_developments.impact: Use is concentrated in writing, coding, and analysis — physical and interpersonal work see far less AI involvement.
